per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA permits that processing may include transferring data to third parties, while the REG text concerns when personal data may be processed except for storage. The clauses do not clearly express the same specific constraint, and no direct quote-supported conflict is established.

The REA mentions that transfer may include transfer to third parties, while the REG text appears to concern disclosure of recipients to the data subject. These are not clearly the same obligation, so no quote-supported mismatch can be concluded.

The REA states that data transfer is required to fulfil processing purposes and may include transfers to third parties, while the REG states a lawfulness condition for processing necessary for contract performance. The texts do not directly conflict on the same constraint.
